summary: Small codegen-focused edits in ftCommon damage code matched `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_Damage_Coll` for GALE01, with symbol-map relabeling of nearby `.sdata2` constants. The title also names `ftCo_Damage_Anim`, but the available diff and decomp.dev report only directly confirm new matches for `OnExitHitlag` and `Coll`.
what_changed: `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` was rewritten to use `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` instead of direct `gobj->user_data` access.

Removed the local `static bool isPointInCircle(float x, float y, float radius)` helper and inlined the stick-magnitude threshold check using `VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick) >= SQ(p_ftCommonData->sdi_min_stick_mag)`.
Introduced an explicit local `bool isPointInCircle` before the existing `if (isPointInCircle || ftCo_800DF608(fp))` branch, preserving a codegen-relevant conditional shape.
`ftCo_Damage_Coll` was changed from `Fighter* fp = gobj->user_data;` to `Fighter* fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj);` while leaving the ground/air collision logic intact.
`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` relabeled `.sdata2` constants `lbl_804D84D4`, `lbl_804D84D8`, `lbl_804D8500`, and `lbl_804D8508` as `@217`, `@218`, `@522`, and `@523` respectively.
decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code at 55.49%, +0.02% and +804 bytes, with 2 new matches: `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` from 99.90% to 100.00% and `ftCo_Damage_Coll` from 99.97% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Inlining the squared stick-length comparison avoided a helper function abstraction that was semantically simple but apparently wrong for matching the target code shape.
Keeping an explicit boolean temporary for `isPointInCircle` likely helped reproduce the original branch/register structure instead of relying on a single compound expression.
Using the project macro `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` made the fighter pointer load both stylistically canonical and matching-friendly.
The PR was intentionally narrow: only two source hunks and one symbol-map hunk were needed to finish the reported function matches.
Relabeling anonymous `.sdata2` constants to `@NNN` symbols avoided overcommitting meaningless `lbl_804D...` names where local/generated constant labels were sufficient.
decomp_lessons: For close fighter callback matches, do not assume a helper function is harmless; the compiler-visible expression shape can matter more than C-level factoring.
Stick magnitude checks should prefer established macros like `VEC2_SQ_LEN` and `SQ` when they match the codebase's expected expansion.
When a target branch appears to materialize a boolean before use, an explicit local `bool` can be a useful matching tactic.
Prefer `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` over raw `gobj->user_data` in fighter code when either style is possible; it can affect matching and follows project convention.
Symbol-map-only changes in `.sdata2` can be part of a matching PR, especially when constants should be represented as generated/local `@NNN` labels rather than named global labels.
No struct or header changes were present in this slice.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_Damage_*` names are used for common fighter damage callbacks such as `ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag` and `ftCo_Damage_Coll`.
`GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` is the canonical macro form for retrieving `Fighter*` from a `Fighter_GObj*` in these source hunks.
Unresolved helper functions remain address-style names such as `ftCo_800DF608`, `ftCo_8008FC94`, `ft_800848DC`, and `ft_80081DD4`.
Anonymous or generated `.sdata2` constants can be named with `@NNN` entries, as shown by `@217`, `@218`, `@522`, and `@523`, instead of `lbl_804D...` labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace a helper call with the exact macro-expanded arithmetic expression needed by the target: `VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick) >= SQ(p_ftCommonData->sdi_min_stick_mag)`.
Use an explicit `if/else` assignment to a `bool` temporary when a direct compound condition does not match.
Swap direct `gobj->user_data` access to `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` to change the emitted code while preserving source-level meaning.
Keep unrelated collision dispatch code unchanged when only the fighter pointer load is needed to match.
Adjust `.sdata2` symbol ownership/names alongside source changes so constants line up with the matched functions.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback confirmed 2 new GALE01 matches and the matched-code increase.
Evidence for `ftCo_Damage_Anim` is weak in this slice: it appears in the PR title, but no source hunk or decomp.dev new-match row confirms it.
follow_up_queries: Check whether `ftCo_Damage_Anim` was affected only by `.sdata2` symbol relabeling or was already matched before PR 2243.
Search other ftCommon callbacks for `gobj->user_data` loads that may need `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` for matching.
Look for stick-magnitude checks factored into helpers where `VEC2_SQ_LEN` plus an explicit boolean temporary may improve codegen.
Audit nearby `ftCo_Damage` `.sdata2` constants to ensure `@NNN` versus `lbl_804D...` naming reflects actual symbol ownership.
